channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.gematsu.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.gematsu.com

item.title: ONTOS delayed to 2027
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5
  • Xbox Series

No longer due out in 2026.

Publisher Kepler Interactive and developer Frictional Games have delayed science-fiction mystery thriller ONTOS from its previously planned 2026 release window to 2027. It will be available for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, and PC via Steam and Epic Games Store.

“ONTOS is our most ambitious game to date, both in terms of its size and scale, and in the depth and layers of our story and gameplay,” Frictional Games said. “In order to deliver this vision, we have decided to push the release of ONTOS to 2027. We look forward to sharing more updates about ONTOS, our characters, and our world soon.”

Watch a clip announcing the delay below.

2027 Delay Announce



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/07/ontos-delayed-to-2027
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Simulation and rhythm game K-POP Rising: Dream to Shine announced for PS5, Switch, and PC
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5
  • Switch

Due out on November 5.

Publisher Microids and developer Old Skull Games have announced simulation and rhythm game K-POP Rising: Dream to Shine for PlayStation 5, Switch, and PC (Steam). It will launch on November 5.

Here is an overview of the game, via Microids:

About

In K-POP Rising: Dream to Shine, players immerse themselves in the demanding and fascinating world of modern music. Accompanied by their best friend Nova, they will need to commit 100 percent to the race for stardom: from intensive training sessions in the studio to the ultimate recognition on the most prestigious stages, the title offers a complete experience combining a sense of rhythm, deep customization, and community management in the era of social media.

  • ❮
  • ❯

Key Features

  • The International Phenomenon in Your Hands – Draw inspiration from your idols and experience the rise of today’s biggest K-Pop groups. Become the face of this culture that fascinates the entire world.
  • An Adventure That Reflects Who You Are (Full Customization) – Express your uniqueness! Create and customize your character from head to toe to match your style and the vibe of your performances. Trendy outfits, iconic makeup… everything is possible to establish your style in the spotlight.
  • Your Group, Your Rules – You call the shots. Create a group in your own image, recruit its members, and turn your very first fans into an international community of millions of followers.
  • Stay in the Rhythm! – Talent is nurtured. Perfect your singing, master highly precise choreographies, and sharpen your sense of timing through captivating rhythm gameplay phases to become an accomplished star.
  • From Your Bedroom to Conquering Stadiums – Start at the bottom of the ladder in the anonymity of your bedroom, climb the ranks through your passion and perseverance, and transform yourself into the international icon of your dreams.
  • Shine Solo, Triumph as a Trio! – Because music is all about sharing, experience this adventure alone or with two friends. Form the group of your dreams in co-op mode and unite your talents to lead your Band straight to the top of the global charts!

Watch the announcement trailer below. View the first screenshots at the gallery.

Announce Teaser Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/07/simulation-and-rhythm-game-k-pop-rising-dream-to-shine-announced-for-ps5-switch-and-pc
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Biped 2 version 2.0 update now available
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS4
  • PS5
  • Switch
  • Xbox One
  • Xbox Series

Significant improvements and enhanced performance.

META Publishing and developer NExT Studios have released the version 2.0 update for cooperative puzzle adventure game sequel Biped 2, which features improved early levels, new puzzles, “better” cooperative play, and enhanced performance when played on Switch 2.

Here is an overview of the update, via META Publishing:

All 17 levels have been significantly overhauled with new puzzle mechanics, ranging from those inspired by old Japanese game shows to classic logic puzzles to chaotic games of tag with elemental creatures. Each puzzle plays significantly differently in co-op than when you go it alone, forcing players to communicate to overcome each challenge.

Over the past months, the development team has focused on refining gameplay, addressing technical issues, and improving the overall quality of the experience, especially on Switch. Numerous bugs have been fixed, level geometry and camera behaviour have been polished, and several gameplay systems have received adjustments based on community feedback. Network stability has been further improved, reducing connection-related disruptions and allowing players to focus on teamwork and puzzle-solving. The team has also continued to evaluate player feedback regarding gameplay balance and progression, reintroducing several familiar mechanics and making targeted adjustments to improve the overall flow of the adventure.

The Version 2.0 update represents another important step in the ongoing support of Biped 2, with the team remaining committed to listening to the community and delivering further improvements across all supported platforms. More visual updates will also be arriving in the coming months.

Biped 2 is available now for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, PlayStation 4, Xbox One, Switch, and PC via Steam, Epic Games Store, and GOG.

Watch a new trailer below.

Version 2.0 Update Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/07/biped-2-version-2-0-update-now-available
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Doloc Town launches August 6
item.content:encoded:
• PC

Currently in Early Access.

Post-apocalyptic pixel art side-scrolling farming adventure game Doloc Town will leave Early Access and launch for PC via Steam on August 6, publisher Logoi Games and developer RedSaw Games Studio announced.

Doloc Town first launched in Early Access on May 8, 2025.

Here is an overview of the version 1.0 release, via Logoi Games:

About

Set in a vibrant yet crumbling world, Doloc Town invites players to start from scratch, rebuild civilization one crop at a time, and uncover the mysteries buried beneath the ruins. Players farm, craft, and build in a post-apocalyptic landscape where acid rain and scorching heat threaten their crops, explore dangerous wilderness with customizable drone companions, and form bonds with the quirky townspeople of a community fighting to survive. With its hand-drawn pixel-art visuals blending the cozy charm of Stardew Valley with the grit of a world in decay, Doloc Town offers a farming adventure unlike any other.

Since launching into Steam Early Access on May 8, 2025, Doloc Town has earned overwhelmingly positive reviews on Steam with an outstanding 95% rating. Over the course of four major updates – Pastoral Waves, Thrive & Ties, Harvest Fusion, and the recent Steam Workshop integration – the game has expanded dramatically, adding animal husbandry, fish farming, townie friendship storylines, a gene system, new town festival, and full mod support. The 1.0 release will mark the culmination of that journey, tying together every system and storyline into the complete Doloc Town experience.

As a last reminder, Doloc Town‘s Early Access price will increase on July 3 at 12:00 a.m. PT / 3:00 a.m. ET—players looking to jump in at the current price have only hours left.

What’s Coming in the 1.0 Full Release

  • New Area: Old City Ruins – A brand-new area to explore, featuring fierce new enemies, new NPCs to meet, new side quests to take on, and new resources to discover.
  • Main Story Conclusion – The post-apocalyptic narrative reaches its finale, delivering the conclusion players have been building toward since Early Access.
  • Farming Automation – A new system that lets players automate key aspects of their farm operations, streamlining workflows and freeing up time for exploration and story.
  • Expanded Language Support – Japanese, Korean, Russian, French, German and Portuguese Brazilian localizations now available alongside English, Simplified Chinese, and Traditional Chinese.

…and much more to be revealed at launch. Stay tuned for the full feature breakdown when Doloc Town 1.0 releases on August 6.

Watch a new trailer below.

Version 1.0 Release Date Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/07/doloc-town-launches-august-6
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: The Alters DLC ‘Last Variable’ gameplay trailer
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5
  • Xbox Series

Due out on July 13.

11 bit studios has released a gameplay trailer for The Alters paid downloadable content “Last Variable,” which is due out on July 13.

Here is an overview of the content, via 11 bit studios:

About the Expansion

“Last Variable” is an expansion to The Alters, in which Jan Scientist awakens from cryosleep with a singular purpose: to solve the mystery of the Oasis. This 20-hour expansion deepens the game’s personal survival mechanics and thought-provoking science-fiction narrative—qualities that earned the original a DICE Award for Strategy/Simulation Game of the Year and a BAFTA nomination for Best Narrative.

Years have passed since Jan Scientist refused to leave the planet. The world has transformed around him. And he realizes what he’s always feared: this work cannot be done alone. So he begins creating again—versions of himself shaped by different expertise. A Geologist. A Biologist. A Chemist. A Physicist. Together, they cover every possibility that needs to be studied.

Across years-long planetary cycles, Jan must Terraform barren sectors into habitable zones, extract and refine exotic substances, and unlock research tiers to push the boundaries of scientific understanding. Yet discovery comes at a cost – the planet responds with escalating threats: earthquakes, radiation surges, and the passage of time itself. Field Labs offer the given Alters refuge to conduct research during surface expeditions, but ageing without cryosleep’s protection remains inevitable.

As his team struggles under the weight of differing philosophies—each convinced their own vision of science is correct—Jan faces an impossible choice: will they fracture under the pressure, or conduct research that could define a legacy?

Key Features

  • The Next Story Focuses on Jan Scientist(s) – Jan Scientists, with their perfectly aligned minds, can offer the expertise needed to unravel the phenomenon of the Oasis.
  • Explore New Regions and Terraform Them – Each transformation shifts the landscape, spawns new resources, and awakens the planet’s defensive mechanism – more frequent earthquakes, severe radiation waves, and other threats.
  • Expand Underground and Survive Cycles – Build in different soil types, construct new components, and research new modules like the Cryosleep Chamber, which will buy you more time and let you witness the long-term effects of your scientific actions.
  • Develop New Tech and Set Field Labs – Each planetary cooldown offers a brief window to explore a dramatically changed surface, and Field Labs introduces new assignments for alters to continue their work while you cryosleep.

The Alters is available now for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, and PC via Steam, Epic Games Store, and GOG.

Watch the trailer below.

DLC “Last Variable” Gameplay Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/07/the-alters-dlc-last-variable-gameplay-trailer
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: The Game Business: Obsidian Entertainment among Xbox studios negotiating to avoid closure
item.content:encoded:
Xbox price increase reportedly thrown together the same day as Apple increase.

The Outer Worlds and Grounded developer Obsidian Entertainment is among the studios negotiating with Xbox to avoid closure, according to a The Game Business report.

Update 10:43 a.m.: Bloomberg reporter Jason Schreier has posted to Bluesky, “Despite a report this morning, I can confirm that Obsidian is not in negotiations to avoid shutting down. Plenty of details are still up in the air surrounding the layoffs (picture will be clear on Monday), but Xbox is keeping Obsidian, according to people familiar with the situation.”

Obsidian Entertainment joins studios including Arkane Studios, Compulsion Games, Double Fine Productions, Ninja Theory, and Undead Labs, all of which have been previously reported as facing potential closure or spinoff.

According to the report, February-appointed Xbox CEO Asha Sharma has brought a “start-up mentality” into the organization, asking teams that are used to weeks of planning and countless meetings to turn things around in days to hours.

The announcement of Xbox Series price increases last week was reportedly thrown together the same day, after Apple announced its own price increases.

Certain Xbox studio heads also feel Sharma is listening too much to consultants rather than the developers making the games.

Numerous people within Xbox who spoke privately to The Game Business feel there is too much “listening to Twitter” happening at Xbox, questioning the organization’s reinvestment in hardware amid an ongoing component crisis and a declining console market, and doubting the impact of an exclusive Gears of War: E-Day on hardware sales.

“We’re chasing a declining market with franchises that are past their prime,” said one studio boss.

Another studio boss, when asked about Xbox’s recent decisions, shrugged and said, “After Activision, this is Amy [Hood] and Satya [Nadella]’s Xbox now.”



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/07/the-game-business-obsidian-entertainment-among-xbox-studios-negotiating-to-avoid-closure
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
